report_name: Marcus & Millichap Hit by Phishing Attack, Limited Data Accessed

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Attacker
threat_actors.description: The unidentified threat actor responsible for the phishing attack against Marcus & Millichap that compromised an employee's credentials to gain unauthorized access to an internal system.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Used a phishing email with a malicious link to steal an employee's credentials for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Used a compromised employee account to gain unauthorized access to a company system.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: Accessed and potentially exfiltrated a limited set of data, including company forms, marketing materials, and contact information from a compromised system.

identities.name: Marcus & Millichap, Inc.
identities.description: A commercial real estate brokerage firm that was the victim of a phishing attack, leading to a data breach of non-sensitive materials.

infrastructure.name: Phishing Website
infrastructure.description: A malicious website was used to harvest an employee's login credentials after they clicked a link in a deceptive email.

indicators.name: Suspicious User Logon Activity
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious User Logon Activity
id: 2b8a1c3d-f4e5-4b6c-8d9a-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects suspicious user login patterns, such as logins from unusual geographic locations or impossible travel scenarios, which could indicate a compromised credential.
references:
  - https://www.marcusmillichap.com/news-events/press-releases/marcus-millichap-releases-information-regarding-cybersecurity-incident
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-12
tags:
  - attack.t1078
  - attack.defense_evasion
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate user travel
  - Use of VPNs
level: medium
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    RiskLevelDuringSignIn: 'high'
    RiskState: 'atRisk'
    RiskEventType: 'impossibleTravel'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects suspicious login activity, such as from unusual geographic locations or impossible travel scenarios, which may indicate the use of stolen credentials as described in the attack.

indicators.name: Network Traffic to Uncategorized or Newly Registered Domains
indicators.pattern:
title: Network Traffic to Uncategorized or Newly Registered Domains
id: 9e8f7a6b-5c4d-4b3a-2e1f-0d9c8b7a6e5d
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects network traffic to uncategorized or newly registered domains, which are often used for phishing websites.
references:
  - https://www.marcusmillichap.com/news-events/press-releases/marcus-millichap-releases-information-regarding-cybersecurity-incident
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-12
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate traffic to new websites
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    category: 
      - 'Newly Registered Domain'
      - 'Uncategorized'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to newly registered or uncategorized domains, a common characteristic of phishing sites used for credential harvesting.

relationships.source_name: Network Traffic to Uncategorized or Newly Registered Domains
